name: api-spectral
description: API specification linting and security validation using Stoplight's Spectral with support for OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, and Arazzo specifications. Validates API definitions against security best practices, OWASP API Security Top 10, and custom organizational standards. Use when: (1) Validating OpenAPI/AsyncAPI specifications for security issues and design flaws, (2) Enforcing API design standards and governance policies across API portfolios, (3) Creating custom security rules for API specifications in CI/CD pipelines, (4) Detecting authentication, authorization, and data exposure issues in API definitions, (5) Ensuring API specifications comply with organizational security standards and regulatory requirements.
qualityReason: Specific tool for API security validation
capabilities: Validate OpenAPI and AsyncAPI specifications against security rulesets
Enforce OWASP API Security Top 10 compliance across API portfolios
Create custom security rules for organization-specific standards
Generate CI/CD integration workflows for automated API security scanning
Map security findings to OWASP API1-API10 categories with remediation guidance
author: AgentSecOps
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing YAML configuration files and markdown guides for API security validation using Spectral. No executable scripts exist. All static findings are false positives triggered by example code patterns in documentation, YAML rule definitions that detect security issues (not use them), and CI/CD template placeholders.
valueStatement: APIs often expose security vulnerabilities through misconfigured specifications. Spectral validates OpenAPI and AsyncAPI definitions against OWASP API Security Top 10, catching authentication flaws, authorization issues, and data exposure risks before deployment.
userTitle: Validate API specs against security standards
limitations: Spectral must be installed separately via npm or Docker
Only validates API specification files (YAML/JSON), not runtime API behavior
Custom rules require YAML configuration knowledge
Does not perform authentication or authorization testing against live APIs

useCases.title: Validate API designs
useCases.description: Check OpenAPI specifications for security issues before writing implementation code

useCases.title: Enforce security standards
useCases.description: Create and distribute organization-wide API security rulesets across development teams

useCases.title: Automate security scanning
useCases.description: Integrate API specification validation into CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions or GitLab CI

promptTemplates.title: Quick security scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the api-spectral skill to scan my OpenAPI specification at [file path] for security issues. Report findings grouped by severity and map each to OWASP API Security Top 10 categories.

promptTemplates.title: Create custom ruleset
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the api-spectral skill to create a custom Spectral ruleset that requires HTTPS on all endpoints, prohibits HTTP Basic authentication, and mandates rate limiting headers for my API specifications.

promptTemplates.title: CI/CD integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the api-spectral skill to generate a GitHub Actions workflow that validates all OpenAPI files in api-specs/ directory against my OWASP ruleset on every pull request.

promptTemplates.title: Remediation guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the api-spectral skill to analyze my Spectral scan results and provide specific remediation steps for each finding, including corrected YAML examples and references to relevant OWASP guidelines.

bestPractices: Integrate Spectral scanning in pre-commit hooks to catch issues before code review
Start with error-severity rules only and progressively add warnings to reduce noise
Version control your rulesets and require peer review for rule changes
antiPatterns: Running Spectral only occasionally instead of on every API specification change
Ignoring informational findings without reviewing their security context
Using overly permissive rulesets that flag everything as a warning

faq.question: What specifications does Spectral support?
faq.answer: Spectral validates OpenAPI 2.x, 3.x, AsyncAPI 2.x, and Arazzo 1.0 specifications in YAML or JSON format.

faq.question: How do I install Spectral?
faq.answer: Install via npm: npm install -g @stoplight/spectral-cli or use Docker: docker pull stoplight/spectral

faq.question: Can I integrate Spectral into my CI/CD pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes, Spectral supports JSON output and GitHub Actions format. Use --format json for machine output and --fail-severity error to fail builds.

faq.question: Does this skill execute any code on my system?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides documentation and YAML templates only. You must install and run Spectral separately using npm or Docker.

faq.question: Why am I getting false positives?
faq.answer: Reduce severity threshold with --fail-severity error initially. Use rule overrides to exclude specific paths or endpoints that have legitimate exceptions.

faq.question: How is this different from API security testing tools?
faq.answer: Spectral validates API specifications (design-time), while tools like ZAP or Burp test running APIs (runtime). Use both for complete coverage.
